top_signals.title: Oil Is Little Changed as Persian Gulf Flows Near Pre-War Levels
top_signals.summary: Oil prices were little changed in thin trading as the recovery of Persian Gulf flows fomented a new surplus in key parts of the global market.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: China steps up claims over sea east of Taiwan - Financial Times
top_signals.summary: China steps up claims over sea east of Taiwan  Financial Times
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: ESMA warns many prediction market event contracts already face EU retail ban
top_signals.summary: The European regulator said companies cannot circumvent EU financial rules by marketing binary-style products as event contracts rather than derivatives.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Spyware found on phone of European Parliament member probing it
top_signals.summary: Stelios Kouloglou, formerly a member of the European Parliament's committee investigating abuses of commercial spyware, was twice infected with Pegasus while serving, researchers said.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Colombia's Oil and Gas Reserves Keep Shrinking
top_signals.summary: For a decade, Colombia's economically vital oil industry has been caught in a death spiral. A combination of sharply weaker oil prices, rising geopolitical risk and anti-petroleum industry reforms implemented by Colombia's first leftist president Gustavo Petro deterred investment, causing the operational tempo to decline. The main issue weighing heavily on the oil patch's outlook is a dire lack of proven petroleum reserves. These are the result of a lack of spending on wildcat drilling and a poor exploration success rate. After a lengthy delay,…
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

top_signals.title: Worse Than an Axis
top_signals.summary: The informal alignment of U.S. adversaries is dangerous.

top_signals.title: Europe Goes Its Own Way
top_signals.summary: Drifting from America, the continent Is rearming and reordering itself.

top_signals.title: Supreme Court decision threatens EU-US data transfer agreement
top_signals.summary: In a Tuesday letter, Max Schrems, the founder of the Vienna-based privacy advocacy organization noyb, told European officials he plans to sue to invalidate the EU-U.S. Data Privacy Framework (DPF) that allows for the transfer of personal data from the EU to U.S. companies.
